proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.1pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Regime stability is 'shifting' with only 1 day in Bear — cohort activation could reverse quickly if regime flips back to Choppy
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance per D-8 review; underlying edge may degrade if exit policy changes
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +41.1pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +41.1pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Regime is now Bear (day 1, shifting) so cohort is active, but TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental risk and current Bear exposure is only 2/11 (18%), while the flagged concentration is on Choppy (81.82%) — promotion does not compound it. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-15) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime = Choppy (81.8%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime = Choppy; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy and in signal_type=UP_TRI/UP_TRI_SA until Choppy share drops below 0.75 via natural Day 6 exits; if adding, prioritize Bear-regime or non-directional setups to rebalance the long-heavy book.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind the entire 11-position long-only book simultaneously with no statistical justification — the dominant Choppy×UP_TRI exposure has no validated cohort edge (only Candidate tier), so an adverse regime transition has no edge buffer to absorb the drawdown.

evidence: Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort at Candidate tier only (n=188) — unvalidated edge
Three concentration cells (UP_TRI_SA×Choppy×IT, UP_TRI×Choppy×Other, UP_TRI×Choppy×Pharma) each at 18.18% — just below 0.20 cell threshold but clustering risk
Book is fully directional long (UP_TRI + UP_TRI_SA + BULL_PROXY = 11/11)
UP_TRI-family share at 9/11 = 81.8%, close to 0.85 signal threshold
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy and in signal_type=UP_TRI/UP_TRI_SA until Choppy share drops below 0.75 via natural Day 6 exits; if adding, prioritize Bear-regime or non-directional setups to rebalance the long-heavy book.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind the entire 11-position long-only book simultaneously with no statistical justification — the dominant Choppy×UP_TRI exposure has no validated cohort edge (only Candidate tier), so an adverse regime transition has no edge buffer to absorb the drawdown.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration is triggered at 0.8182 (Choppy, above 0.80 threshold) with the matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort only at Candidate tier (n=188) — unvalidated edge. The book is also fully directional long (UP_TRI + UP_TRI_SA + BULL_PROXY = 11/11), and combined UP_TRI-family share sits at 9/11 = 81.8%. No single cell breaches the 0.20 threshold (top three cells at 18.18%), but the regime concentration with unvalidated cohort support warrants warn.
